titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Russell Hoban Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 1, 2021 at 03:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Russell Hoban Papers, which span from 1952 to 2011, contain writings from throughout Russell Hoban's lifetime including novels, children's books, poetry, and opera librettos. The papers also contain clippings, ephemera, photographs, audiovisual materials, and electronic media related to Hoban's life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 68, 74, 77, 86-87 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 69, 73, 78, 85 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Jockey Hollow Fund and on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Writings, 1956-2011. II. Correspondence, 1963-2011. III.Writings on Russell and Lillian Hoban, 1964-2002. IV. Diaries, 1956-2005. V. Artwork, circa 1952-1980.

bioghist.head: Russell Hoban (1925-2011)
bioghist.p: Russell Hoban (1925-2011) was an American author who wrote novels, poetry, operatic librettos, and children's books. Notably, he collaborated with illustrator and author Lillian Hoban, to whom he was married from 1944-1972, on the "Frances the Badger" children's books.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Russell Hoban Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Russell Hoban Collection. Related materials: Lillian Hoban Papers (GEN MSS 1199). Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, drawings, posters, correspondence, other papers, and audiovisual materials related to the life and work of Russell Hoban. The writings and drawings document Hoban's career as a novelist, librettist, and children's book author including the Frances books he collaborated on with his former wife, Lillian Hoban.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Russell Hoban Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to Russell Hoban's writings. Materials within this series are organized under seven headings: children's books, scripts and librettos, poems, short stories, essays and short fiction, novels, and unsorted notes, writings, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: What Does it Do and How Does It Work?--original sketches
What Does it Do and How Does It Work?--proof copy
The Story of Hester Mouse--notes and drafts
The Tin Bulldog & the Brown Sparrow
Three O'Clock Place--drafts, notes, and pictures
Claudia and the King of the Wood--early notes and drafts
Claudia and the King of the Wood--drafts
Claudia and the King of the Wood--drafts
Claudia and the King of the Wood--Chapters 1-7
A Thread to Find--unpublished children's book and notes
Egg Thoughts and other Frances Songs
La Corona and the Tin Frog
Victorian Inventions postcard--inspiration for Captain Najork
How Tom Beat Captain Najork and His Hired Sportsmen; A Near Thing For Captain Najork
The Dancing Tigers and Other Savage Stories
The Dancing Tigers and Other Savage Stories
Aunt Fidget Wonkham-Strong Najork's Monster Rally
Always Say Hello to the Twelfth Chinese Boy
Random notes and drafts for children's books
The Sea-Thing Child--final print copy for reprint
A Bed Time for the Scalybums
Jim Hedgehog and the Lonesome Tower
Jim Hedgehog and the Pantomime Horse Dept.
The Ups and Downs of Adam Mole
Jim Hedgehog artwork--exchange with Jane Nissen
The Court of the Winged Serpent
The Court of the Winged Serpent
The Secret of the Emerald Key
The Ghost Horse of Genghis Khan
Illustration notes for "The Great Fruit Gum Robbery"
The Ghost of Jimson Crow--drafts and notes
Turtle Diary: A Book at Bedtime
Mouse and His Child--notes and letter
People of the Forest--libretto, drafts, notes
Mouse and His Child--screenplay by Carol Mon Pere
Saturday with Miss Daphne and Lemon Drops
The Mouse and His Child--adaptation by Tamsin Oglesby
By the River in the Mind--radio play
The Second Mrs. Kong--reviews, cuttings, libretto, notes
The Second Mrs. Kong--programs, letters, scores
The Vermeer Girl and Kong--drafts, notes, letters
Some Episodes in the History of Miranda and Caliban
The Hynd of the Wood photocopy
Dragons Made of Gingerbread and other poems
The Space Voyage of Drlzi Nngh
I Could Win the Title and other poems
The Fountain Lady and other poems
The Ageing Lover and other poems
Admission: One Shilling and other poems
I Saw Two Stone Lions and other poems
My Lost Noah's Ark--poem and notes
Poetry pre-1967 plus unused "Pedalling Man" material
My Trip with Arabella and other stories
Like a Circle in a Spiral
The Man with the Dagger--Granta version
The Man with the Dagger--draft, story
Dionysis at the Ball Park--notes, sketches
Introduction/foreword to Walter De La Mare's "Missing"
Opening address to the Walter De La Mare Society
"Wilde Pomegranates" essay in Children's Literature in Education
On the Bearable Beastliness of Being
"1975"--essay about time, place, and Riddley Walker
Essay on Walter De La Mare
Draft of Walter De La Mare speech
Article on working with Impact Theatre Group
The Moment Under the Moment--essay, sketches
Essay on the radio and music
A Little as Plaining about Riddley Walker
Mnemosyne, Teen Taals, and Tottenham Court Road
Pan Lives, By the Way at the Inn, Portknockie
Stories That Grew Up With Me
Puffin Post feat. essay on speaking objects
Recent thoughts on being and writing
Lecture on awareness and artistic representation
Lecture on learning to read--for children lit conference
Rose Hip Tea at Cranks and other sketches
Article about "The Mouse and His Child"
On Machinery and the Morality of Beauty and other drafts
The Mouse and His Child--original artwork

accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Timecode is visible. Occasional scratches, dropouts and dirt are visible. The frame's edge can also be seen at times. Program is silent throughout the tape.

did.materialspec: The time code is visible. Typical attributes of film transfer are present including occasional scratches, dirt and dropouts. The frame's edge is also visible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Apple IIe is contained within its original carrying case. The keyboard keys are imprinted with Arabic letters, either painted on or engraved. It's possible these modifications are related to Russell Hoban's 1983 novel Pilgermann.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Russell Hoban and many other authors, composers, and creatives, along with ephemera sometimes included with the correspondence. The material documents personal and business relationships related to Russell Hoban.

did.unittitle: Letter to the Times about Harrison Birtwhistle
Correspondence with Dorothy Wood at Penguin
Letters to Anthony Whitworth-Jones re: The Second Mrs. Kong"
Correspondence with Anthony Whitworth-Jones and others
Correspondence with Ann and Anthony Waite
Correspondence with Michael Sporn and John Rush
Correspondence with Arthur Levine and others
Correspondence with Gilles de la Mare
Correspondence with Gillon Aitken and Jeremy Payton
Writings on Russell and Lillian Hoban

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains articles and other writings related to the lives and works of Russell Hoban and Lillian Hoban.

did.unittitle: "Literature in Altered Englishes"--dissertation by Franci Celoria
Study on Hoban's books for University of Zurich
"Is There Hope of Anything?"--dissertation by Geneve Van Osch
Harper and Row leaflet featuring Russell and Lillian Hoban

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains calendar books annotated by Russell Hoban.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this series maintain the original organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains artwork created by Russell Hoban.
